title: Issues running nvidia runtime rootless
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On two RHEL 7.7 workstations, I have installed podman, the latest nvidia driver from El Repo, and installed the nvidia-container-toolkit. I also implemented the following: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3659#issuecomment-543912380. On one workstation, this has allowed me, both as root and rootless, to run the nvidia/cuda image successfully. On the other workstation, after following the same steps, I am getting the following:

```
$ podman run --privileged --rm -it --hooks-dir /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d nvidia/cuda nvidia-smi
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/nvidia/cuda...ERRO[0001] Error pulling image ref //registry.access.redhat.com/nvidia/cuda:latest: Error initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/nvidia/cuda:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/nvidia/cuda: name unknown: Repo not found 
Failed
Trying to pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 6001e1789921 done
Copying blob 251f5509d51d done
Copying blob 8e829fe70a46 done
Copying blob 47b91ac70c27 done
Copying blob 35c102085707 done
Copying blob 9f0a21d58e5d done
Copying blob 23bff6dcced5 done
Copying blob 2137cd2bcba9 done
Copying blob a0529eb74f28 done
Copying config 946e78c7b2 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:430: container init caused \"process_linux.go:413: running prestart hook 0 caused \\\"error running hook: exit status 1, stdout: , stderr: nvidia-container-cli: initialization error: driver error: failed to process request\\\\n\\\"\""
```

I am not sure what is different about this machine that is causing this issue?


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Installed El Repo nvidia driver

2. Installed podman

3. Installed nvidia-container-toolkit

4. Followed https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3659#issuecomment-543912380

5. Ran $ podman run --privileged --rm -it --hooks-dir /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d nvidia/cuda nvidia-smi as a non-root user

**Describe the results you received:**
Container reports back the following error:

```
Error: container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:430: container init caused \"process_linux.go:413: running prestart hook 0 caused \\\"error running hook: exit status 1, stdout: , stderr: nvidia-container-cli: initialization error: driver error: failed to process request\\\\n\\\"\""
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
```
# podman run --rm --privileged --hooks-dir /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d nvidia/cuda nvidia-smi
Wed Nov 13 22:01:17 2019       
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| NVIDIA-SMI 430.50       Driver Version: 430.50       CUDA Version: 10.1     |
|-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
| GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |
| Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |
|===============================+======================+======================|
|   0  GeForce GTX 108...  Off  | 00000000:17:00.0 Off |                  N/A |
|  0%   29C    P8     7W / 250W |      2MiB / 11178MiB |      0%      Default |
+-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
|   1  GeForce GTX 108...  Off  | 00000000:65:00.0  On |                  N/A |
|  0%   41C    P8    17W / 250W |    392MiB / 11175MiB |      1%      Default |
+-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
                                                                               
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Processes:                                                       GPU Memory |
|  GPU       PID   Type   Process name                             Usage      |
|=============================================================================|
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+

```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Again, this process worked on one machine but not another.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:

  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.7"
  MemFree: 3467210752
  MemTotal: 33223602176
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 16479416320
  SwapTotal: 16479416320
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: hostname.domain
  kernel: 3.10.0-1062.4.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 5h 56m 22.64s (Approximately 0.21 days)

  blocked: null
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
rpm -q podman nvidia-container-toolkit
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
nvidia-container-toolkit-1.0.5-2.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: The nvidia hooks are not likely to work with rootless.  Please contact nvidia, we would love to work with them on fixing the issue.  Bottom line,the rootless user is not allowed to do any privileged functions like creating device nodes or load kernel modules.
@rhatdan do we need to get this documented somewhere?
Okay, thank you very much for the explanation. It's just a little strange since I've been able to run it rootless on a few other machines, but not the machine in question on this ticket.

@Ngpriddy I would figure the devices were created and kernel modules were loaded on the other machine, so the Nvidia plugin did not have to do anything on these machines.  I would guess if you rebooted those machines and ran rootless first, you would see similar errors.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
